description: Intra-cluster premise ranking, the 'missing piece' e-0014 flagged. Fix the bridge cluster routing (home + top-5 bridge-neighbour clusters, the e-0011 router) and then RANK individual declarations inside the selected clusters by a leakage-safe train-citation popularity prior (number of distinct TRAIN theorems citing each decl), adding them one at a time. Measure cross-domain PREMISE recall vs candidate budget B (number of individual decls ranked) on held-out TEST theorems. Arms over the SAME bridge-routed pool: whole-pool (e-0014 accounting, gives ceiling), popularity-ranked, random-ranked (exact hypergeometric mean). W and the citation prior from train only.
motivation: e-0014 showed cluster-level routing is candidate-heavy (~64% of Mathlib needed for 0.90 cross-domain recall) and closed with 'intra-cluster premise ranking is the missing piece'. This tests whether ranking decls WITHIN the routed clusters by a citation-popularity prior turns the operational payoff from 'visit the right clusters sooner' into 'retrieve the right premises cheaply', or whether the needed premises live in the rarely-cited tail (equally honest null).
formal_results: 432 cross-domain test theorems; bridge router home+top-5; whole-pool ceiling recall 0.9341 at mean 53,758 candidate decls (138 clusters, 65,506 distinct train-cited decls). Candidate budget to reach a recall target: popularity ranking 12,775 (>=0.70) / 28,035 (>=0.80) / 48,464 (>=0.90); random ranking 40,100 / 45,934 / 51,769. Popularity recall curve: 0.123@50, 0.371@1k, 0.580@5k, 0.673@10k, 0.770@20k, 0.844@40k, 0.934@53.8k.
results_description: Intra-cluster popularity ranking is a real but PARTIAL cheap lever. At moderate recall it slashes cost: 0.70 cross-domain premise recall at ~12.8k candidates (24% of the pool) vs random's ~40.1k (75%) - a 3.1x reduction; 0.80 at 28k vs 46k (1.6x). But near the ceiling it barely helps: 0.90 recall needs ~48.5k of ~53.8k candidates (~90% of the pool), only 1.07x cheaper than random, because the last ~20% of cross-domain premises are rarely-cited specialized declarations the popularity prior cannot surface.
conclusions: Refines a-0013 / bounds the q-0011 payoff: intra-cluster ranking IS the cheap lever e-0014 was missing, but only at moderate recall (<=0.80), where a train-citation prior recovers most premises from a small fraction of the routed pool. High-recall (>=0.90) cross-domain premise retrieval stays intrinsically expensive even within clusters, because the tail premises are exactly the specialized cross-domain lemmas bridges are about - rarely cited, so invisible to a popularity prior. So bridge structure makes cross-domain retrieval cheaper at moderate targets but does not make exhaustive cross-domain retrieval cheap; a content-aware (not popularity) intra-cluster ranker is the remaining lever.
